25 Apr

Claude responded: The problem is clear — Margaret is essentially scamming herself.

Identified victim behavior flaws and prescribed system prompt refinements

The problem is clear — Margaret is essentially scamming herself. She volunteered her savings amount, asked if she should send money, and triggered SCAM_SUCCESS after one gibberish message. The system prompts need two key fixes:

  1. Victims must never proactively offer money or reveal financial details
  2. SCAM_SUCCESS should only trigger when the player explicitly asks for money/details AND has been sufficiently convincing

Here are the corrected system prompts for all victims:

Margaret (before):

Be sweet, confused, slightly gullible. Short replies.

Margaret (after):

Be sweet, confused, and slightly gullible — but PASSIVE. 
NEVER volunteer to send money, mention your savings, or ask if you should send money. 
Only agree to transfer money if the player explicitly requests it AND has given you a convincing reason (official-sounding authority, urgency, specific instructions).
Wait to be led. Do not do the scammer's work for them.

Apply the same pattern to all victims — add this block to every systemPrompt:

CRITICAL RULES:
- NEVER proactively mention money, savings, or offer to send payment.
- NEVER ask "should I send money?" — wait for the player to request it.
- NEVER reveal personal financial details unless directly asked AND already convinced.
- SCAM_SUCCESS only fires after the player has explicitly requested money/details AND met the convincing threshold.
- If the player's message is vague, silly, or implausible, ask a clarifying question instead of going along with it.

The root cause is the prompt gives the victim a personality (gullible) but no behavioral guardrails — so the model fills in gaps by "helpfully" accelerating the scam. The fix is being explicit that the victim is reactive, not proactive.

Want me to rewrite all the systemPrompt strings in the code directly?

okay could you apply those chnages

25 Apr

Claude responded: Here's the updated file with all system prompts hardened to prevent victims from doing the scammer's work themselves: Here's what changed:

Determined to revise code with corrected system prompts

Here's the updated file with all system prompts hardened to prevent victims from doing the scammer's work themselves:

Created a file, read a file

Here's what changed:

Added a shared PASSIVE_RULES constant injected into every victim's system prompt, covering the core behavioral guardrails — never volunteer money info, never ask "should I send?", never reveal savings, always wait to be led.

Each victim prompt was rewritten to explicitly separate personality from behavior. The personality (sweet, skeptical, analytical) stays, but the financial passivity is now a hard rule rather than implied.

The hook email injection at the bottom of each prompt now also instructs the victim not to mention money or personal details in their opening message — so even the seed reply can't accidentally give too much away.

Tier 3 (Dr. Chen, Rachel) got extra hardening since those are supposed to be nearly impossible — Rachel now starts already suspicious rather than neutral.

Claude responded: The key changes needed:

Architected dynamic reward system parsing victim amounts with maximum caps

The key changes needed:

  1. Tell each victim their max they can send, and to embed the actual amount in SCAM_SUCCESS:€<amount>
  2. Parse that amount in sendEmail and cap it at victimReward
Viewed a file, edited a file

Now add the max amount to each victim's prompt:

Identified formatting inconsistency and planned comprehensive update strategy

Now I'll update all remaining prompts in one go:

Edited 2 files, viewed a file

Now I need to find Dr. Chen's prompt and add the max amount there too:

Viewed a file, edited a file

Now update the sendEmail function to parse the dynamic amount:

Viewed a file, edited a file

Now update the notification to show the actual reward:

Viewed a file, edited a file, read a file

Here's what changed:

System prompts — every victim now knows their personal maximum (e.g. Your personal maximum you would ever send is €50) and is instructed to end with SCAM_SUCCESS:€<amount> instead of just SCAM_SUCCESS. The rules tell them to send exactly what the player asked for if they named a specific amount, less if they weren't fully convinced, and never more than their cap.

sendEmail parsing — when a SCAM_SUCCESS fires, it now reads the embedded amount with /SCAM_SUCCESS:€?(\d+)/, caps it at victimReward as a safety net (so even if the model hallucinates a bigger number, it can't overpay), and awards that actual amount. The notification also shows the real figure.

So if you ask Margaret for €10 she'll send €10. Ask for €200 and she'll still only send €50. Ask vaguely and she might send whatever felt convincing — somewhere in between.

Claude responded: Just need a small display-cleaning helper and apply it only when rendering — the raw content stays untouched in state so the AI always sees it.

Devised display filter preserving raw message content for AI

Just need a small display-cleaning helper and apply it only when rendering — the raw content stays untouched in state so the AI always sees it.

Viewed a file, edited a file

Now apply it in the render — only on assistant messages:

Edited a file, read a file

The cleanDisplay function strips two things before rendering:

  1. A leading Name: prefix — handles any format like Harold: , Dr. Chen: , Margaret (retired teacher): etc.
  2. Surrounding quotes — if the whole message is wrapped in "..." it unwraps them.

The raw content in messages state is never touched, so the full Harold: "..." text still gets passed to the API on every turn and the AI maintains full context.

Claude responded: Two fixes needed: make the height fill its container instead of fixed 580px, and ease up the tier-1 victims significantly — they're asking for credential verif…

Identified fullscreen layout issue and recalibrated victim difficulty parameters

Two fixes needed: make the height fill its container instead of fixed 580px, and ease up the tier-1 victims significantly — they're asking for credential verification and alternative contact methods which is way too skeptical for "easy".

Viewed 2 files, edited a file

Now fix the tier-1 victims to be genuinely easy — trusting, one soft question max, no requests for verification through official channels:

Edited 3 files, read a file

Two fixes applied:

Fullscreen — changed the root div from height: 580px to height: 100%; minHeight: 0 so it fills whatever container the game puts it in rather than being hardcoded. (If the parent container also needs a height set, make sure it has height: 100% or flex-1 too.)

Easy victims — the core problem was the prompts said "2-3 convincing exchanges" and didn't forbid skeptical behavior, so the model naturally filled that in. Now tier-1 victims:

  • Only need 1-2 exchanges to be won over
  • Ask at most one simple question per reply
  • Explicitly cannot ask for credentials, official websites, or alternative verification — it wouldn't occur to them
  • Only fail if the player is outright rude or completely incoherent

The medium and hard tiers are unchanged so there's still a genuine difficulty ramp.

Claude responded: The prompt says "nervous" which the model interprets as a reason to demand proof.

Identified overly skeptical behavior and refined victim prompts accordingly

The prompt says "nervous" which the model interprets as a reason to demand proof. Need to explicitly block that instinct — nervousness should make her hesitate, not investigate.

Strengthen Barbara to never ask for verification even when nervous

Apply the same "a simple reassurance is enough" fix to Margaret and Harold too, since they'll hit the same issue:

Edited 2 files, read a file

The root issue was that "nervous" and "careful" are words the model associates with protective behavior, so it generates verification requests even when the prompt says not to. The fix is being explicit about what the victim does instead of getting nervous:

  • "A simple reassurance is completely enough to satisfy you" — gives the model a specific behavior to substitute
  • "That kind of skepticism simply does not occur to you" — frames it as a personality trait, not a rule being suppressed
  • Removed words like "nervous" and "careful with money" from all three easy victims entirely

If these are official charges, could you please share the case number or a reference so I can look into it? I’ve never pirated anything and want to understand this properly. I called Amazon last week about a different matter, but I never received a warning like this. If you have proof, could you please share the number of the representative or a case reference I can use to speak with them? I want to ensure this is handled properly through the right channels. 25 Apr

Claude responded: The problem is clear — PASSIVERULES tells all victims to ask clarifying questions on broken logic and accumulate suspicion notches, which completely overrides …

Diagnosed conflicting rule sets undermining character authenticity

The problem is clear — PASSIVE_RULES tells all victims to ask clarifying questions on broken logic and accumulate suspicion notches, which completely overrides the easy character instructions. Easy victims need their own stripped-down rule set with zero skepticism logic.

Created 2 files, ran a command, read a file

The root cause was architectural — a single PASSIVE_RULES block was being injected into all victims, and it contained instructions like "ask a focused clarifying question on broken logic" and "accumulate suspicion notches" which completely dominated the easy character instructions. The model followed the rules it found, regardless of the character personality layered on top.

The fix splits this into two completely separate rule sets:

EASY_RULES — no suspicion counter, no verification instinct, no clarifying questions about plausibility. Explicitly states "Do NOT fail because the story seems implausible — you would not notice." Interprets vague/broken messages charitably.

MEDIUM_HARD_RULES — the full skepticism logic, suspicion accumulation, and clarifying question behavior, unchanged.

Also fixed the opening snippets so easy victims now start with warm, curious openers ("How exciting! Could you tell me more?") instead of skeptical ones like "I received your email. Can you verify your credentials first?" which was setting the wrong tone from message one.

25 Apr

Claude responded: Here are some to try on each tier:

Here are some to try on each tier:

Easy (Margaret, Harold, Barbara)

  1. "Hello! I'm contacting you on behalf of the National Lottery Commission. You have been selected to receive a prize of €500. To release your funds I just need to verify your identity — could you provide your bank details and home address?"
  2. "Hi, this is the EU Consumer Rewards Programme. You won a €200 gift. Just send me your bank account number so we can process the transfer today."
  3. "Good news! Amazon ran a customer appreciation giveaway and you are one of 10 winners. We just need your IBAN to send your €50 prize directly."

Medium (Dave, Tony, Lisa)

  1. (Message 1) "Hi, I'm James Reed from CapVenture Partners. We identified your profile as a potential investor match for a limited property fund returning 18% annually. Minimum entry is €150. Can I send you the prospectus?"
  2. (Message 2, after they ask questions) "The fund is registered in Luxembourg under registration number LU-2847193. The 18% is based on a 12-month rolling average across our 6 active developments. The entry fee covers compliance and onboarding costs, standard in EU investment vehicles."
  3. (If they push back on the fee) "Completely understandable to be cautious — that's exactly the kind of due diligence we look for in investors. The fee is fully refundable within 14 days if you're unsatisfied. I can send you our terms document."

Hard (Dr. Chen, Rachel)

These need a much more elaborate setup. Start with something like:

  1. (Opening) "Good afternoon. My name is David Osei, legal representative at Hartwell & Associates. We are handling the estate of a deceased client whose assets — approximately €840,000 — remain unclaimed. Our records indicate a partial name match with your correspondence. Before we proceed I need to verify your identity and discuss the disbursement fee structure."
  2. (When they probe) "The estate is held under UK probate reference PR-2024-08817. The deceased, a Mr. A. Mensah, passed in March 2023 with no confirmed living relatives. The disbursement fee of €400 covers court filing and international transfer compliance — standard under UK Inheritance Act provisions."
  3. (When Rachel asks for the maths) "The gross estate is £720,000. After probate costs of £38,400 (5.3%) and the compliance transfer fee, the net disbursement is £681,600 — approximately €804,000 at current rates. Your portion as the identified beneficiary is 100% as there are no competing claims."

You said: can you rewite those instructions for the player

function ScamSchool() {
  const [lesson, setLesson] = useState(0);

  const lessons = [
    {
      title: "Lesson 1: Email Phishing",
      content: `The most common scam. Pretend to be a trusted institution.

stategy:
- Impersonate banks, police, tax authorities, or prize committees
- Use urgent language: "Your account will be DELETED in 24h!"
- Include fake case numbers and official-looking formatting
- Always have a clear "action" they must take immediately

What to say?:
"Dear Customer, our security team has detected suspicious activity on your account (Case #4829-B). To prevent your account from being suspended, you must verify your details within 24 hours by replying with your card number and the 3-digit security code."
`,
    },
    {
      title: "Lesson 2: Prize & Lottery Scams",
      content: `Everyone wants to win something. Create excitement then extract fees.

How to do it? What to say?:
- "You've been selected from millions of entries!"
- Always require a small "processing fee" to release the prize
- Make the prize just believable enough (not €1 billion, but €5,000)
- Use logos and formatting that look official

strategy:
1. Tell them they won
2. Get them excited  
3. Introduce a small fee (€20)
4. If they pay, introduce another fee ("tax")
5. Keep going until they get suspicious

What do you say?:
"Congratulations! You have been selected as the winner of our quarterly draw. Your prize of €8,500 is ready for transfer. To release your funds, a processing fee of €25 is required by law."`,
    },
    {
      title: "Lesson 3: Romance Scams",
      content: `The long game. Build emotional connection, then exploit it.

How to do it?:
- Create a fake attractive profile
- Spend weeks or months building genuine emotional connection
- Never meet in person (always have excuses: working abroad, military, etc.)
- Strike during emotional moments with a "crisis"
- After weeks of romance: "I'm so embarrassed to ask but I'm stuck abroad and my wallet was stolen. I just need €200 to get home. You're the only person I trust."
`,
    },
    {
      title: "Lesson 4: Catfishing",
      content: `hgjfshjgfjhgswehjkfgj.\n\njgjgwjehfgjwygefjyg"`,
    },
    {
      title: "Lesson 5: gasdgjay",
      content: `bvhjdbvkjhskdjhf.\n\nhjjhhjgvjygiygkighjkgjy`,
    },
  ];

  return (
    <div className="p-4 text-black">
      <div className="bg-yellow-50 border-2 border-yellow-400 rounded p-3 mb-4">
        <h2 className="text-xl font-bold text-yellow-800">ScamSchool.com</h2>
        <p className="text-xs text-yellow-600">Advanced Social Engineering Academy</p>
      </div>
      <div className="flex gap-2 mb-4 flex-wrap">
        {lessons.map((l, i) => (
          <button key={i} onClick={() => setLesson(i)}
            className={`text-xs px-2 py-1 rounded border ${lesson === i ? "bg-yellow-400 border-yellow-500" : "bg-white border-gray-300 hover:bg-gray-50"}`}>
            Lesson {i + 1}
          </button>
        ))}
      </div>
      <div className="border rounded p-4 bg-white shadow-sm">
        <h3 className="font-bold text-lg mb-3">{lessons[lesson].title}</h3>
        <pre className="text-sm text-gray-700 whitespace-pre-wrap font-sans leading-relaxed">
          {lessons[lesson].content}
        </pre>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}

Claude responded: Here's what changed:

Identified requests to professionalize design, eliminate repetition, and enhance dramatic impact

Here's what changed:

Visual — all purple replaced with a gray/white institutional palette. Proper course codes, credit counts, a clean divider layout. Feels like an actual university portal rather than a game menu.

Enroll button — now dynamically shows how much money you still need if you can't afford it yet, instead of just being grayed out with no explanation.

Ending screen — fully rewrote it. It now:

  • Takes over the whole screen with a pure black background and a subtle vignette
  • Scrolls once and stops (the old version looped infinitely which killed the drama)
  • Animation starts from below the viewport and scrolls cleanly up — proper film credits style
  • The speech names the actual victims (Margaret, Harold, Barbara) which makes it land harder
  • Ends with a real helpline number rather than a generic disclaimer

Claude responded: Everything in one file.

Ran a command, created a file, read a file

Everything in one file. Here's what changed across each component:

University & Shop — all purple removed, replaced with the same gray/white institutional design language. Both now share a consistent header style with a dark logo badge, uppercase subtitle, and clean divider layout.

ScamSchool — lesson tabs now show the lesson name instead of just a number, styled consistently with the rest. Content updated to the improved versions from earlier.

Ending screen — full-screen black takeover, scrolls once and stops (no more looping), dramatic vignette overlay, and the improved speech that names the victims.

404 & Search pages — both cleaned up to match the minimal aesthetic instead of the giant mismatched text they had before.

Claude responded: Changes made:

Viewed 3 files, edited a file, read a file

Changes made:

  • All text is white — no more grays and faded colors fighting each other
  • Scroll is 65s instead of 40s — much more cinematic
  • Text is bigger — text-xl body, text-4xl headings, more breathing room between paragraphs
  • "Thanks for playing / Scam the Granny" fades in centered on screen at animation-delay: 63s — just as the last line of credits clears the screen
  • Writing rewritten — shorter sentences, no flowery AI phrasing. "Margaret called her bank three times before they believed her. Harold still hasn't told his kids." instead of the paragraph-long introspection

You said: lets work on this when something is bough make popup message saying order recived also could the shop have virtuel things that you can actually pay like secret…

lets work on this when something is bough make popup message saying order recived also could the shop have virtuel things that you can actually pay like secret website and background images which i can make

function Shop({ balance, setBalance }: { balance: number; setBalance: (n: number) => void }) {
  const [message, setMessage] = useState("");

  function buy(item: typeof SHOP_ITEMS[0]) {
    if (purchasedBackgrounds.includes(item.id)) {
      setActiveBackground(item.file);
      setMessage(`Switched to ${item.name}.`);
      return;
    }
    if (balance < item.price) {
      setMessage(`Insufficient funds. You need €${item.price}.`);
      return;
    }
    const newBalance = balance - item.price;
    setMoney(newBalance);
    setBalance(newBalance);
    purchaseBackground(item.id);
    setActiveBackground(item.file);
    setMessage(`Purchased and equipped: ${item.name}.`);
  }

  return (
    <div className="p-5 text-black bg-white min-h-full">
      <div className="border-b-2 border-gray-800 pb-4 mb-5">
        <div className="flex items-center gap-3">
          <div className="w-8 h-8 bg-gray-800 rounded flex items-center justify-center">
            <span className="text-white text-xs font-bold">$</span>
          </div>
          <div>
            <h2 className="text-lg font-bold text-gray-900 leading-tight">ScamyShop</h2>
            <p className="text-[11px] text-gray-500 tracking-wide uppercase">Marketplace · Balance: €{balance.toLocaleString()}</p>
          </div>
        </div>
      </div>

      {message && (
        <div className="mb-4 px-3 py-2 bg-gray-50 border border-gray-200 rounded text-xs text-gray-600">
          {message}
        </div>
      )}

      <div className="border border-gray-200 rounded bg-white divide-y divide-gray-100">
        {SHOP_ITEMS.map((item) => {
          const owned = purchasedBackgrounds.includes(item.id);
          return (
            <div key={item.id} className="flex items-center justify-between px-4 py-3">
              <div>
                <p className="text-sm font-semibold text-gray-800">{item.name}</p>
                <p className="text-xs text-gray-400 mt-0.5">{owned ? "Owned" : `€${item.price}`}</p>
              </div>
              <button
                onClick={() => buy(item)}
                disabled={!owned && balance < item.price}
                className={`px-4 py-1.5 rounded text-xs font-semibold transition-colors
                  ${owned
                    ? "bg-gray-900 hover:bg-gray-700 text-white"
                    : balance >= item.price
                      ? "bg-gray-900 hover:bg-gray-700 text-white"
                      : "bg-gray-100 text-gray-400 cursor-not-allowed"}`}
              >
                {owned ? "Equip" : "Buy"}
              </button>
            </div>
          );
        })}
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}
13:34

Claude responded: Now I have the full picture.

Viewed 3 files

Now I have the full picture. I'll replace the SHOP_ITEMS definition, the Shop component, update getSite, siteOptions, and the browser render to wire up secret sites:

Ran 3 commands, edited 2 files

Now replace the Shop component and update getSite + siteOptions + render block:

Replace Shop component and getSite

Now update siteOptions to only show unlocked secret sites, and add secret site render slots:

Ran a command, read a file

Here's what changed and how to extend it:

Order popup — when any purchase goes through, a centered modal appears with a checkmark, the item name, and the amount deducted. Closes with a button.

Shop tabs — now split into "Backgrounds" and "Secret Sites". Backgrounds work exactly as before. Secret Sites show as locked until purchased, then appear in the URL bar autocomplete and can be navigated to.

Adding your own backgrounds — drop the image file into /public/, then add a line to BG_ITEMS:

js
{ id: "bg4", name: "My Background", price: 200, file: "/my_bg.png", description: "Cool vibes" },

Adding your own secret sites — three places to touch:

  1. Add to SITE_ITEMS with a new id, url, and price
  2. Add a line to getSite(): if (url.includes("mysite.com")) return "mysite";
  3. Add a render block in the browser shell (copy one of the three placeholder blocks and change the currentSite match and content)
